Standardize names across benchmark replicates

Source: R/benchmark.R
standardize_benchmark_names.Rd

Reads benchmark_records.csv, pools all unique names from specified columns, sends each to an LLM for standardization, and adds standardized columns to the CSV. Name mappings are cached to name_mappings_{label}.csv to avoid re-running the LLM on subsequent calls.

Usage

standardize_benchmark_names(
  benchmark_dir,
  name_columns = list(list(column = "Host_Name", type = "host", std_name =
    "Host_Name_std"), list(column = "Pathogen_Name", type = "pathogen", std_name =
    "Pathogen_Name_std")),
  model = NULL
)

Arguments

benchmark_dir

Directory containing benchmark_records.csv

name_columns

A list of lists, each with:

column

Column name in the CSV to standardize

type

Name type for the LLM prompt ("host" or "pathogen")

std_name

Name for the new standardized column

model

LLM model to use for standardization (default: Claude via ellmer)

Value

The updated records tibble (also overwrites benchmark_records.csv)

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Default host-pathogen schema
records <- standardize_benchmark_names("data/test_variability")

# Custom schema
records <- standardize_benchmark_names(
  "data/test_variability",
  name_columns = list(
    list(column = "bat_species", type = "host",
         std_name = "bat_species_std"),
    list(column = "organism", type = "pathogen",
         std_name = "organism_std")
  )
)
} # }
